questions.q: Who are the parents of Kusha?
questions.explanation: Kusha was one of the twin sons born to Rama and Sita.

questions.q: Kusha had a twin brother. What was his name?
questions.explanation: Kusha's twin brother was Lava, the other son of Rama and Sita.

questions.q: In whose hermitage were Kusha and Lava raised?
questions.explanation: The twins were raised in the ashram of the sage Valmiki, author of the Ramayana.

questions.q: What great work did Kusha and Lava recite and sing?
questions.explanation: The twins recited the Ramayana, the epic about their own father, which Valmiki taught them.

questions.q: The Ramayana that Kusha recited was about which king?
questions.explanation: The twins sang the story of Rama, who was unknowingly their own father.

questions.q: Besides song, in what were Kusha and Lava said to be skilled?
questions.explanation: The twins were trained as skilled warriors in arms in addition to being gifted singers.

questions.q: Where did Sita live when she gave birth to Kusha and Lava?
questions.explanation: Sita, banished from Ayodhya, lived in Valmiki's hermitage where the twins were born.

questions.q: Kusha and Lava are best known as the first reciters of which epic?
questions.explanation: They were the first to publicly recite Valmiki's Ramayana.

questions.q: What was the family lineage (dynasty) of Kusha through his father Rama?
questions.explanation: As Rama's son, Kusha belonged to the Ikshvaku or Solar dynasty of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Kusha and Lava performed the Ramayana at which event organized by Rama?
questions.explanation: The twins sang the Ramayana before the assembly at Rama's Ashvamedha sacrifice.

questions.q: How did Rama come to realize that Kusha and Lava were his own sons?
options: Their recited epic told his life story and they resembled him
Valmiki announced it at the coronation
They defeated him in single combat
questions.explanation: Rama recognized the truth as the boys sang his story and bore a striking resemblance to him.

questions.q: According to tradition, which city did Kusha rule as king?
options: Kushavati (in the south of the Kosala kingdom)
questions.explanation: Kusha is said to have ruled from Kushavati, a city associated with his name.

questions.q: Which kingdom did Rama divide between Kusha and Lava?
questions.explanation: Rama divided the Kosala kingdom, giving each twin a portion to rule.

questions.q: In the traditional division, which region did Lava receive while Kusha took the southern Kosala?
options: The northern Kosala, ruling from Shravasti
questions.explanation: Lava ruled northern Kosala from Shravasti, while Kusha ruled the southern portion from Kushavati.

questions.q: Valmiki taught the twins to sing the Ramayana in what musical form?
questions.explanation: Valmiki composed the epic in shlokas and trained the boys to sing it melodiously.

questions.q: When the twins sang the Ramayana before Rama, they refused offered gold because they were what?
options: Ascetics living in a hermitage with no need for wealth
questions.explanation: As hermitage-dwelling ascetics, the twins declined material reward for their recitation.

questions.q: What is the relationship of Kusha to Lakshmana?
options: Lakshmana is his teacher in the ashram
questions.explanation: Lakshmana, Rama's younger brother, is the uncle of Kusha and Lava.

questions.q: Who is the maternal grandfather of Kusha through his mother Sita?
questions.explanation: Sita was the daughter of King Janaka, making him Kusha's maternal grandfather.

questions.q: Who is the paternal grandfather of Kusha through his father Rama?
questions.explanation: Rama was the son of Dasharatha, who is therefore Kusha's paternal grandfather.

questions.q: The Uttara Kanda, the section of the Ramayana describing the twins, is which book of the epic?
questions.explanation: The story of Kusha and Lava appears in the Uttara Kanda, the seventh book of the Ramayana.

questions.q: During the Ashvamedha, before recognizing them, how did Rama react to the twins' singing?
options: He was deeply moved and listened day after day
He demanded they stop after one verse
questions.explanation: Rama and the assembly were captivated and had the twins continue the recitation over many days.

questions.q: According to a popular folk etymology, the name 'Kusha' is linked to what object Valmiki used at the twins' birth?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Valmiki named the twins after the kusha grass and lava (a bit) he used in a protective rite.

questions.q: In the Uttara Kanda, when Rama's sacrificial horse was seized, which warrior captured it, leading to conflict with Rama's army?
questions.explanation: In Valmiki's account it is Lava who first confronts the horse's guard, and the twins together fight Rama's forces.

questions.q: Which of Rama's brothers was defeated or subdued by the twins when he came to recover the sacrifice horse?
options: Lakshmana (along with Bharata and Shatrughna in turn)
questions.explanation: In the Uttara Kanda the twins overcome Rama's brothers, including Lakshmana, before Rama himself intervenes.

questions.q: In some traditions, the city of Lahore in Pakistan is associated with which son of Rama?
questions.explanation: Folk tradition links the founding of Lahore (Lavapuri) to Lava, while Kasur is associated with Kusha.

questions.q: Through whom did Valmiki convey the twins' true parentage so that Sita could be summoned to the assembly?
options: Valmiki sent word vouching for Sita's purity before Rama
questions.explanation: Valmiki personally testified to Sita's purity and the twins' identity, asking Rama to accept her.

questions.q: After Sita returned to the earth, who took on the role of raising and guiding the twins toward their kingdoms?
options: Rama, who brought his sons to Ayodhya
questions.explanation: After Sita's departure, Rama acknowledged the twins and prepared them to inherit the kingdom.

questions.q: According to the Raghuvamsha of Kalidasa, Kusha's line continued the dynasty after which event concerning Ayodhya?
options: Ayodhya was temporarily deserted and later restored by Kusha
Ayodhya was given to the vanaras
questions.explanation: Kalidasa relates that Kusha restored and reoccupied a deserted Ayodhya, continuing the Raghu line.

questions.q: In the Raghuvamsha, Kusha is said to have married a princess of which type after a divine encounter?
options: Kumudvati, a Naga princess of the river
questions.explanation: Kalidasa's Raghuvamsha describes Kusha marrying Kumudvati, sister of a Naga king.

questions.q: The poetic style of reciting the Ramayana as a chanted ballad, as Kusha and Lava did, is preserved in which traditional term?
options: Kushilava, a term for wandering bards/reciters
questions.explanation: The word 'kushilava,' meaning a bard or reciter, is traditionally derived from the names Kusha and Lava.
